summary: Ukrainian forces reported intensified UAV and missile activity targeting Poltava's energy infrastructure and the city of Almaznyi. Russian troops claimed control over eastern Konstantinovka and liberated villages Okhrimovka (Kharkiv region) and Roskoshnoe (DPR). Both sides continued air and artillery strikes across multiple fronts, including heavy attacks on civilian structures in Belgorod region and bridge damage near Armianska.
key_points: Ukrainian air defence radar detected ballistic missile trajectories toward Poltava and Almaznyi, indicating a focused strike on energy assets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
BBS 67 OMBR "Zalizni sokoly" conducted personnel elimination operations near Temirivka (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Ukrainian artillery used BM-21 "Grad" rockets on the village of Belaya Berezka in Bryansk region, damaging administrative buildings and residential houses (Source: @tass_agency/380273).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced liberation of Okhrimovka in Kharkiv region and Roskoshnoe in the DPR (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Ukrainian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Donetsk directions (Source: @kpszsu/64871, @kpszsu/64843).
Ukrainian forces struck four bridges near the Armianska crossing into Crimea, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81853).
Russian forces reported full control of the eastern part of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/380291).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian missile and UAV activity targeted Poltava's energy infrastructure, while Russian forces claimed control of eastern Konstantinovka and liberated villages Okhrimovka and Roskoshnoe. Both sides intensified artillery and aerial bomb strikes, affecting civilian structures in Bryansk and bridge infrastructure near Armianska. The conflict remains volatile with multiple fronts experiencing active engagements.

points: Ukrainian air defence reported a ballistic missile heading toward Poltava and Almaznyi, suggesting a coordinated strike on regional energy facilities (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
BBS 67 OMBR "Zalizni sokoly" carried out personnel elimination operations near the settlement of Temirivka, indicating ongoing special‑operations activity (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).

Ukrainian artillery employed Grad multiple‑rocket launchers on the settlement of Belaya Berezka in Bryansk region, damaging the local administration building and ten multi‑apartment and three private houses (Source: @tass_agency/380273).
Ukrainian forces reported striking four bridges in the Armianska area at the entrance to Crimea, disrupting Russian supply routes (Source: @UkraineNow/81853).

Ukrainian troops claimed to have hit a railway worker in Konotop with a reactive Shahed drone, causing one fatality and injuries to four railway staff, plus damage to housing and loss of gas supply (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
Ukrainian air units launched guided aerial bombs against targets in Kharkiv and Donetsk regions, indicating use of precision strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/64871, @kpszsu/64843).

Ukrainian defence reported a single casualty and sixteen injuries in Dnipropetrovsk region after a Russian attack, noting repeated drone and aerial bomb assaults across four districts (Source: @UkraineNow/81835).

Russian Ministry of Defence announced the liberation of the village Okhrimovka in Kharkiv region and the village Roskoshnoe in the DPR, expanding control in contested areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Russian forces declared full control over the eastern part of Konstantinovka, consolidating their hold on the settlement (Source: @tass_agency/380291).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided aerial bomb releases over Kharkiv and Donetsk directions, demonstrating continued air‑strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/64871, @kpszsu/64843).
Russian authorities reported two fatalities resulting from Ukrainian attacks on Bryansk region, indicating civilian impact from cross‑border strikes (Source: @tass_agency/380291).
Russian forces reported the capture of Ochrimovka and the liberation of Roskoshnoe, reinforcing their narrative of offensive progress (Source: @tass_agency/380270).
Russian military statements highlighted the use of Shahed drones approaching Poltava from the north, targeting energy infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67562).
Russian reports indicated that guided aerial bomb attacks were launched against Ukrainian positions in the Donetsk sector (Source: @kpszsu/64843).
Ukrainian forces used Grad rockets on Belaya Berezka in Bryansk, damaging civilian structures near the Russian border (Source: @tass_agency/380273).
Russian troops announced control of eastern Konstantinovka, solidifying their position on the eastern front (Source: @tass_agency/380291).
Ukrainian guided aerial bomb strikes were directed at Kharkiv direction targets, indicating active air operations on the eastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
Russian liberation of Okhrimovka (Kharkiv region) and Roskoshnoe (DPR) reflects shifting front‑line dynamics in the east (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Ukrainian UAV activity over Poltava and Almaznyi, though farther west, may affect logistical routes supporting eastern operations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
Russian tactical aviation continued bomb runs over Donetsk, maintaining pressure on Ukrainian defensive lines (Source: @kpszsu/64843).
Ukrainian special‑operations unit BBS 67 OMBR operated near Temirivka, a location close to the eastern front line (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Chernihiv region (e.g., Nosivka, Bobrovytsia, Liubech) indicating aerial activity in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/96230).
Ukrainian radar detected a Shahed drone approaching Poltava from the north, suggesting possible northern ingress routes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67562).
Russian reports of controlled villages in the Kharkiv region (Okhrimovka) affect the northern sector of the conflict (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Dobrjanka in Chernihiv, showing continued reconnaissance in the north (Source: @eRadarrua/96234).
Ukrainian guided bomb releases over Kharkiv direction may have originated from northern air bases (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
No direct combat casualties reported on the northern front in the provided messages.
Ukrainian UAV activity over Kyiv region (Yahotyn/Berezan) indicates ongoing aerial operations near the capital (Source: @kpszsu/64861).
Ukrainian forces struck four bridges near Armianska at the Crimea entrance, disrupting Russian supply lines in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81853).
Ukrainian attacks on Konotop in Sumy region caused a railway worker death and injuries, with gas outages and housing damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81837).
Ukrainian UAVs reported over Mykolaiv region from the Black Sea, indicating southern maritime aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/64850).
Ukrainian UAVs observed near Balabanivka from the sea, suggesting coastal surveillance (Source: @eRadarrua/96225).
Russian reports of civilian casualties in Bryansk region reflect cross‑border strikes affecting southern Russian territories (Source: @tass_agency/380291).
Ukrainian air defence noted missile trajectories toward Poltava and Almaznyi, which lie in central‑southern Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
Ukrainian special‑operations activity near Temirivka (southern Kharkiv oblast) may influence southern operational depth (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Ukrainian air defence detected ballistic missile approaches to Poltava and Almaznyi, indicating missile launches against Ukrainian airspace (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv and Donetsk sectors, showing continued offensive air operations (Source: @kpszsu/64871, @kpszsu/64843).

Ukrainian UAV activity reported across multiple regions: Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, Donetsk, and over the Black Sea towards Mykolaiv (Sources: @eRadarrua/96193, @kpszsu/64838, @kpszsu/64846, @kpszsu/64850).
Russian Shahed drones approached Poltava from the north, targeting energy infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67562).
Ukrainian artillery employed Grad rockets on Belaya Berezka in Bryansk, demonstrating cross‑border artillery fire (Source: @tass_agency/380273).
Ukrainian guided bomb releases indicate use of precision strike munitions against enemy positions (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
No explicit reports of air superiority shifts; both sides continue to employ UAVs and missile systems.

key_developments: Ukrainian air defence detected a ballistic missile heading toward Poltava and Almaznyi, indicating a focused strike on energy infrastructure (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67549).
BBS 67 OMBR "Zalizni sokoly" conducted personnel elimination operations near Temirivka, showing active special‑operations engagement (Source: @DeepStateUA/23570).
Russian forces announced full control of the eastern part of Konstantinovka, consolidating territorial gains (Source: @tass_agency/380291).
Ukrainian artillery used Grad rockets on Belaya Berezka in Bryansk, damaging administrative and residential buildings (Source: @tass_agency/380273).
Russian Ministry of Defence reported liberation of Okhrimovka (Kharkiv region) and Roskoshnoe (DPR), expanding their front‑line control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30841).
Ukrainian forces struck four bridges near Armianska at the Crimea entrance, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/81853).
Russian tactical aviation launched guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv and Donetsk directions, maintaining offensive air pressure (Source: @kpszsu/64871).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian forces are intensifying missile and UAV attacks on critical energy sites in central Ukraine, while also conducting special‑operations near frontline settlements. Russian troops are consolidating control over key villages and launching guided aerial bombs, indicating sustained offensive capability. Both sides' use of Shahed drones and cross‑border artillery poses heightened risk to civilian infrastructure and logistics corridors.
sections.regional_response: Local authorities in affected Russian regions reported damage to civilian structures and casualties, prompting increased civil defence measures. Ukrainian regional administrations highlighted infrastructure disruptions, such as bridge damage near Crimea and gas outages in Sumy, leading to civilian evacuations and sheltering. International observers note the escalation of cross‑border strikes as a destabilising factor for the broader region.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows a pattern of north‑west ingress toward Poltava and southern maritime routes toward Mykolaiv, suggesting coordinated strike planning. The reported liberation of villages aligns with Russian operational narratives of offensive momentum, but Ukrainian guided bomb usage indicates retained precision strike capability. Continued artillery exchanges across the border underscore the need for reinforced air defence and civilian protection measures.
